60 Phase 11 & 12: Finish the Record

Phase 11: Create the Mastering Plan

¢ Confirm the mix is ready.

¢ Decide what the final master needs: tonal balance, loudness, control, warmth, brightness, glue, and translation. ¢ Use a reference and identify the goal before processing.

Student Rule: A master cannot save a broken mix.

Phase 12: Execute the Master

¢ Apply final EQ, compression, saturation, limiting, stereo control, and loudness adjustment.

¢ Check the song on monitors, headphones, phone, and other speakers. ¢ Export the final version only after it translates well.

Student Rule: Master with intention, then test in the real world.

=~ —»—— Final Takeaway = —— =

‘ )\\ The Checkmark Production Blueprint Method turns a blank session \ into a finished professional song by following a clear order: study, map, prepare, create, arrange, refine, polish, mix, and master.

> Blueprint Song Itt Mix follows Master [Z| complete arranged aplan {9) translates well
